title: rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. patulus
intro:
Rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. patulus, also known as the parasol or yellow rattle, is a plant species in the broomrape family that obtains its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants, specifically grasses.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of iridoids and flavonoids.

The health benefits associated with Rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. patulus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, as well as its possible antiviral and antibacterial effects.

Herbal preparations of Rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. patulus can be made in the form of tinctures, infusions, or decoctions, and are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
